title: caluera surinamensis
intro: Caluera surinamensis, also known as the Guianese caluera, is a plant species native to the Guiana Shield in South America, specifically in the countries of Suriname, Guyana, and French Guiana. This plant is recognized for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Caluera surinamensis include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Caluera surinamensis are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in traditional remedies, such as teas and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
